identity.title: Plastics Packaging Film and Sheet (including Laminated) Manufacturing

lens.included_activities: US lower-middle-market manufacturers that repeatedly supply flexible plastic packaging film or packaging sheet, including laminated plastic-to-plastic structures, to external converters, packagers, and industrial or consumer-goods customers.
lens.excluded_activities: Captive internal film production, pure resin or film distribution, finished bag and pouch conversion, nonpackaging unlaminated film and sheet, paper-foil-plastic combinations classified elsewhere, shells, and non-control financing vehicles.
lens.customer_and_revenue_model: Recurring rolls or sheet sold by weight, area, specification, or supply program, with pricing often influenced by resin indices and differentiation based on gauge, barrier, seal, printability, qualification, recyclability, and delivery reliability.

a.bridge: BLS OEWS reports occupations for broader NAICS 326100, not wage-weighted AI task exposure in 326112. The estimate maps the observed broader mix of extrusion, forming, inspection, engineering, maintenance, logistics, sales, and office roles to exposed tasks in production planning, quality analytics, predictive maintenance, formulation and structure search, quoting, procurement, customer service, and administration. It may overstate exposure if continuous film plants are more automated and production-heavy, or understate it if specialty multilayer products require more engineering and technical service.
a.rationale: Continuous extrusion, coating, lamination, winding, material handling, and maintenance are capital- and equipment-intensive. Information-heavy indirect work and exception handling are exposed, but they represent a minority of wage cost and existing process automation is excluded.
a.change_evidence: A six-digit staffing and wage matrix combined with time studies at blown-film, cast-film, coating, and lamination plants, documenting current controls and task-level hours in operations, quality, engineering, maintenance, planning, and commercial support.
caveats: The occupation evidence is at four-digit plastics manufacturing.
Employment shares do not directly measure wage-weighted task exposure.

rho.rationale: Planning, computer-vision triage, process anomaly detection, maintenance support, specification retrieval, document automation, and quoting can be implemented in five years. Legacy control systems, sparse labeled defect data, costly downtime, resin variability, food-contact and customer validation, cybersecurity, and the need for human process accountability limit full realization.
rho.change_evidence: Plant deployment studies showing baseline and post-implementation labor hours, scrap, line speed, downtime, complaints, and quality escapes for AI-enabled scheduling, inspection, maintenance, and commercial workflows over at least twelve months.
caveats: The cited automation evidence is occupational and not a film-plant AI outcome study.
Benefits may appear as yield or throughput rather than avoidable labor.

e.rationale: Independent specialty film producers can have recurring external supply relationships and transferable assets, but eligibility is reduced by integrated captive capacity, capital intensity, large-company ownership, commodity exposure, outdated extrusion lines, environmental and food-contact diligence, and customer concentration.
e.change_evidence: A complete 326112 firm and plant roster with EBITDA, ownership, captive share, external recurring revenue, product structures, customer concentration, line age and utilization, permit status, qualification transferability, and maintenance capital needs.
caveats: The injected firm count is model-derived.
Flexible-packaging sources include downstream converters and nonplastic substrates outside this code.

s5.bridge: Census ABS owner-age evidence covers employer businesses across industries, not qualifying five-year control transfers among 326112 LMM film manufacturers. The estimate converts broad succession exposure into a transfer probability and discounts internal succession, corporate asset reorganizations, closures, failed processes, and plants without transferable economics. It may overstate transfer frequency in a capital-intensive consolidated sector or understate it among mature family-owned specialty producers.
s5.rationale: Founder succession can create supply, but corporate integration and plant-scale economics reduce the relevance of general small-business ownership data. No comprehensive six-digit closed-deal series was located.
s5.change_evidence: Five years of PitchBook, Capital IQ, GF Data, Axial, broker, bankruptcy, and asset-sale records reconciled to the 326112 roster and classified by control, EBITDA, operating continuity, and buyer type.
caveats: ABS is not observed industry deal flow.
A plant or line sale may not constitute a qualifying transferable firm.

q.rationale: Fixed-price periods and differentiated barrier, seal, downgauged, recyclable, or qualified structures can preserve savings. Resin-index pass-through, transparent material economics, high-volume bids, converter purchasing power, and frequent qualification of alternatives can transfer productivity benefits to customers over time.
q.change_evidence: Contract and invoice analysis by product family covering resin and energy indices, conversion spreads, open-book terms, rebates, bid and renewal cadence, customer concentration, qualification expense, and realized price after productivity improvements.
caveats: No source measures five-year retention of AI-generated benefit.
Specialty multilayer film and commodity monolayer film have materially different bargaining power.

d5.bridge: Census e-commerce sales and Flexible Packaging Association evidence measure demand-adjacent channels and the broader flexible-packaging sector, not constant-quality units of 326112 film and sheet. Growth in shipped goods, food, healthcare, and flexible-format penetration supports volume, while EPA policy direction, lightweighting, reuse, recycled-content mandates, and paper substitution reduce virgin plastic film demand. The translation may overstate volume because nominal sales include inflation and services, or understate share gains from rigid packaging.
d5.rationale: Protective, barrier, and shelf-life functions remain valuable, with flexible packaging used across resilient end markets. Sustainability redesign creates both substitution risk and demand for new mono-material or recyclable films. The base is near-flat-to-modest real growth, with wide downside from policy and material reduction.
d5.change_evidence: Census product-class shipment tonnage by film type and resin, end-market packaging-area forecasts, customer sustainability roadmaps, state EPR fee schedules, recycled-content mandates, and observed substitution to paper, reusable, compostable, or rigid formats.
caveats: Broad flexible-packaging measures cover formats and materials outside 326112.
Revenue and economic impact are not constant-quality quantity.
Policy direction does not equal uniform national implementation.

o.rationale: Surviving demand for film or sheet requires physical resin conversion, layer or coating control, winding, testing, release, and delivery. AI can optimize these operations and software can reduce packaging use, but it cannot supply the remaining physical barrier or wrap. Insourcing and imported film remain more plausible operator displacement paths.
o.change_evidence: Converter and brand-owner sourcing data that separates demand eliminated, downgauged, shifted to alternative materials, imported, or vertically integrated, plus evidence of customer-site autonomous film production.
caveats: The estimate is conditional on quantity remaining after material reduction.
The lens's external operator can be displaced even if film demand persists.

narrative.executive_view: Packaging film and sheet is a capital-intensive repeat-supply business. AI is most credible in planning, process and quality analytics, maintenance, specification handling, and commercial administration, while continuous physical production remains operator- and equipment-dependent. Demand is supported by flexible packaging functions but reshaped by downgauging, recyclability, reuse, and plastic policy.
narrative.ai_impact: Extrusion, coating, lamination, winding, changeovers, maintenance, sampling, and accountable quality release remain physical. AI can assist with recipe and structure retrieval, schedule optimization, process anomaly detection, vision inspection, predictive maintenance, documentation, quoting, and customer service.
narrative.value_capture: Differentiated barriers, sealing performance, food-contact qualification, reliable tolerances, and recyclable designs create switching friction. Commodity film is more exposed to resin-index pricing, transparent conversion spreads, high-volume bids, and customer demands for productivity sharing.
narrative.firm_availability: Transferable independents likely exist, especially in specialty structures, but the nominal count must be screened for integrated captive plants, large-company ownership, outdated assets, maintenance capital, customer concentration, utilization, and qualification portability. General owner-age data are only a weak transaction proxy.
narrative.demand_durability: Food protection, healthcare, industrial wrap, consumer goods, and shipping sustain film functions. E-commerce remains supportive, but lightweighting, reuse, producer responsibility, recycled-content requirements, and paper or other material substitution can reduce virgin-plastic tonnage even when packaged-unit demand grows.
narrative.risks_and_uncertainty: Principal risks are resin and energy volatility, capital intensity, uptime, quality escapes, food-contact compliance, concentrated customers, environmental policy, recycling compatibility, and rapid materials innovation. Broad sector measures poorly identify the exact product mix of an eligible firm.
narrative.principal_driver: Recurring specification-based film demand and rich process data create targeted opportunities in quality, yield, uptime, planning, and commercial workflows.
narrative.principal_weakness: Capital intensity, resin-linked pricing, and policy-driven material reduction can compress the transferable pool and pass operational savings to customers.

sources.title: 2022 NAICS Definition: Plastics Packaging Film and Sheet Manufacturing
sources.supports: Defines 326112 as converting plastic resins into flexible packaging film and packaging sheet and distinguishes adjacent activities.

sources.title: May 2023 OEWS: Plastics Product Manufacturing
sources.supports: Provides broader plastics-product occupation counts, including extruding, forming, inspection, engineering, printing, and other production roles used as an occupation-mix proxy.

sources.title: Metal and Plastic Machine Workers
sources.supports: Describes machine setup, test runs, adjustments, repairs, inspection, monitoring, and automation that allows one operator to control multiple machines.

sources.title: ABS Characteristics of Business Owners: 2022 Tables
sources.supports: Provides age-of-owner tables used solely as a broad succession proxy.

sources.title: Annual Business Survey Methodology: 2022
sources.supports: Explains ABS employer-business coverage, sampling, and limitations.

sources.title: 2025 Flexible Packaging Achievement Awards
sources.supports: Shows current innovation around recyclable flexible structures, barrier performance, product protection, and reduced environmental impact.

sources.title: U.S. Flexible Packaging Industry Economic Impact
sources.supports: Describes flexible packaging's broad use across agriculture, food, healthcare, and e-commerce and its 2025 US economic footprint.

sources.title: Quarterly Retail E-Commerce Sales: First Quarter 2026
sources.supports: Reports $326.7 billion of seasonally adjusted first-quarter 2026 e-commerce sales, 9.8% above a year earlier and 16.9% of retail sales, as a demand-adjacent indicator.

sources.title: National Strategy to Prevent Plastic Pollution
sources.supports: Documents the federal strategy's focus on reducing and recovering plastics and preventing pollution, while noting the strategy is under current-administration review.
